LAW AND MOTION 302, DEFENDANTS PAUL CHOW AND ANGIE CHAN'S DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT - THE DEMURRER IS SUSTAINED WITH LEAVE TO AMEND TO ADD RORCE LO AS AN INDISPENSABLE PARTY, AS PLAINTIFFS CONCEDE IN THEIR OPPOSITION THEY INTEND TO DO. AS TO THE BALANCE OF THE DEMURRER, IT IS SUSTAINED WITH LEAVE TO AMEND TO ADD THE LLC ITSELF. DEMURRER IS OVERRULED WITH REGARD TO ADDING THE MEMBERS OF THE LLC AS INDISPENSABLE PARTIES. PLAINTIFF HAS 10 DAYS FROM NOTICE OF ENTRY OF ORDER TO FILE THE AMENDED COMPLAINT. (PREVAILING PARTY TO PREPARE A FORM OF ORDER.) JUDGE: MARLA J. MILLER; CLERK: GINA GONZALES; COURT REPORTER: ANTHONY C. VAUGHN, CSR # 6185

MINI MINUTES:
Appearances:
Jeffrey H. Belote, Esq. of Morris Polich & Purdy LLP, Tel. 415.984.8508, for defendants and moving party;
Timothy S. Kirk, Esq. of Lewis Brisbois Bisgaard & Smith LLP, Tel. 415.262.8536, for plaintiff and opposing party.

RULING: The matter was argued. At the conclusion of argument, the court modified its tentative ruling on DEFENDANTs PAUL CHOW and ANGIE CHAN's DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT as follows: The demurrer is sustained with leave to amend to add Rorce Lo as an indispensable party, as Plaintiffs concede in their opposition they intend to do. As to the balance of the demurrer, it is sustained with leave to amend to add the LLC itself. Demurrer is overruled with regard to adding the members of the LLC as indispensable parties. Plaintiff has 10 days from notice of entry of order to file the amended complaint. (Prevailing party to prepare a form of order.) Judge: Marla J. Miller; Clerk: Gina Gonzales; Court Reporter: Anthony C. Vaughn, CSR # 6185
